sprint: Lumo's Arrival Becomes a Real Video, Not a Static PNG
task: Direct, explicit instruction on top of the just-shipped Gate work: "instead of static png portrait fly in this dragon" -- swapping Lumo's Scene 2 arrival from a static PNG to the real, newly-uploaded assets/video/gateway/lumo-flying.mp4 (isolated Lumo, genuinely pure black background, 5.04s, confirmed via ffmpeg + raw pixel sampling). mountLumo() in js/gatewaySequence.js now plays this video via mix-blend-mode:screen (compositing the black background away) using the existing 1.8s .gateway-lumo-arrive keyframe unchanged, then crossfades to the pre-existing static portrait's own idle bob/blink/wing-flutter system after FLIGHT_MS (4900ms) -- the static portrait and the video are never both visible. Reduced motion skips the video entirely, exactly matching the file's own established discipline. A real gap was found and closed during verification: the new video had no poster fallback (unlike the Gate video's own GATE_POSTER_SRC), so with this sandbox's headless Chromium unable to decode H.264 at all (an already-documented limitation from the Gate video work), nothing painted during the flight window; fixed by extracting a real frame-0 still and wiring it as LUMO_FLY_POSTER_SRC, matching the Gate video's own pattern. A second, disclosed rendering caveat (the poster shows as a visible black box rather than blending transparently in this sandbox specifically, despite mix-blend-mode:screen computing as correctly applied via direct inspection) is recorded as a sandbox/headless-rendering limitation, not a CSS defect -- the settled, crossfaded static-portrait state composites correctly with no such artifact. Verified via a new 10-assertion Playwright suite plus a full re-run of the entire pre-existing 41-assertion Gateway regression suite (three of its own global .play()-call-count assertions re-scoped to a Gate-video-specific counter, since the new Lumo video now also legitimately calls .play()) and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/gatewaySequence.js, css/style.css, and one new poster image asset, with a matching cache-bust version bump on both stylesheet <link> tags in index.html.
